sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.21 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let${ N}_p$denote the formation of all finite$p 0.1ex$-groups, for a given prime number~$p$. Is it true that, for every subformation${ F}$of${ N}_p$, there exists a variety${ M}$such that${ F}={ N}_p { M}$? { A.\,F.\,Vasil'yev} No, it is not true. There is a natural one-to-one correspondence between formations of finite$p$-groups and varieties of pro-$p$-groups: for every variety${ V}$of pro-$p$-g...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
